english: Junction C: The East–West direction receives 14% more demand than North–South, but the fixed signal split gives it insufficient green time; meanwhile, the pedestrian phase is consuming 20% of the cycle, holding back vehicle traffic during peak periods. Recommended action: Reprogram the signal controller to increase green time on the East–West axis (currently insufficient for the 1.14× demand imbalance). Monitor the pedestrian phase; if cross-traffic demand continues to build during peak hours (1200–2400 s window), consider creating a dedicated pedestrian-exclusive phase outside the vehicle green window, or shorten the all-way pedestrian signal. Implement adaptive (time-of-day or demand-responsive) signal timing to shift green allocation as demand patterns change throughout the day. Expected impact: Smart signal timing typically reduces average vehicle wait time by 15–30%, depending on demand profile. At this junction, with sustained peak queues of 91.95 vehicles and 119 s average wait, a 20% reduction would save drivers ~24 seconds of delay per trip.
